title: `taylor`/derivative special forms silently read a stale/corrupted value (or segfault) when their lambda argument closes over a TCO'd self-recursive function's OWN loop-carried parameter
goal: Discovered while implementing ESH-0196 (P11 tower-based user numerics; taylor-ode-solve builds ODE solution coefficients via a self-recursive Scheme loop that calls `taylor` on a freshly-built closure each iteration). When a tail-recursive (TCO'd) Eshkol function F(p1, p2, ...) constructs, INSIDE its own body, a NEW lambda that closes over one of F's own parameters (a PHI-loop-carried variable once TCO'd into a loop) and passes that lambda to `taylor` (and very likely `derivative`/`gradient`/`derivative-n`, which share the same free-variable-capture machinery in lib/backend/autodiff_codegen.cpp), the captured value read inside the tower evaluation is WRONG once the function actually recurses at least once: a captured double reads as unrelated garbage (observed: capturing t=5.0 read back as ~6.17e9); a captured LIST reads as corrupted memory that then crashes downstream list operations (`cdr: argument is not a pair`) or segfaults outright with a 'stack overflow' signal at very shallow (depth ~2) recursion, which is a misleading symptom of memory corruption, not genuine stack exhaustion.
repro: (define (rec t i k) (if (= i k) 99 (let* ((g (lambda (h) (+ t h))) (gc (taylor g 0.0 i))) (display gc) (newline) (rec t (+ i 1) k)))) (rec 5.0 0 1) -- prints a garbage huge number instead of 5.0 for gc's captured `t`, then 99.

Same shape with a captured LIST argument (e.g. `bs` holding ODE coefficients, closed over by `(lambda (h) (f (+ t h) (tn-horner bs h)))`) instead crashes with `cdr: argument is not a pair` or a SIGSEGV reported as a stack-overflow, even at a nominal recursion depth of 1-2.

The SAME closure-over-a-plain-value pattern works correctly when the enclosing function is NOT self-recursive (a plain one-shot function, e.g. `(define (once t) (let* ((g (lambda (h) (+ t h))) (gc (taylor g 0.0 0))) gc))`) -- confirms the bug is specific to TCO'd/loop-carried parameters, not lambda-capture-into-taylor in general.

Two independent workarounds both fix it: (1) breaking strict tail-call position on the recursive call (e.g. wrapping it as `(+ 0 (rec ...))`), or (2) rebinding the captured parameter into a FRESH local `(let ((t2 t)) ...)` immediately before constructing the closure, so `t2` (not the raw loop-carried `t`) is what gets captured. Both point at the PHI-loop representation of `t`/`bs` as the corrupted read, consistent with the known PHI-in-loop codegen bug family already tracked informally in project memory (reference_bugs_fixed.md: 'PHI-in-loops... closure ABI... findFreeVariables').
acceptance: The minimal repro above (captured double AND captured list, self-recursive function, taylor call inside the loop body) produces the CORRECT value with no workaround needed, under both -r and AOT
A regression test is added under tests/ad/ (e.g. taylor_closure_over_loop_param_test.esk) covering both the scalar and list-capture cases

No regression in taylor_tower_test.esk (52), taylor_tower_mono_test.esk (441), exact_taylor_test.esk (68), guw_multivariate_test.esk (35), taylor_numerics_test.esk (41), run_ad_depth.sh, run_ad_oracle.sh (56), run_sicp_smoke.sh (88)

Investigate whether `derivative`/`gradient`/`derivative-n` (which share free-variable-capture logic in autodiff_codegen.cpp per the file's own structure) have the same latent bug even though no failing case has been observed yet for them specifically
workaround_in_place: lib/core/ad/taylor_numerics.esk's tn-ode-coeffs-loop (ESH-0196) rebinds its loop-carried `f`/`t` parameters into fresh locals `f0`/`t0` immediately before constructing the closure passed to `taylor`, with an explanatory comment at the definition site. Verified correct via a minimal, independently reproduced repro (see 'repro' above) before and after the workaround. This workaround is no longer NECESSARY after the fix below (verified: the un-workaround'd shape now produces the correct value), but it was left in place on the P11 branch since that branch (and `taylor`/tower codegen itself) is out of scope for this fix -- `taylor` does not exist on master yet.
related: ESH-0117/ESH-0138 (reverse-over-nested-forward, prior closure/free-variable capture bug in the AD path)
scope_note: `taylor` (and the P1-P11 tower/jet machinery) lives only on feat/ad-taylor-p11-user-numerics and its ancestor branches -- it is NOT merged to master as of this fix, so it could not be used as the repro vehicle here. Per the ticket's own acceptance criterion #4 ('investigate whether derivative/gradient/derivative-n... have the same latent bug'), `derivative` and `gradient` -- which DO exist on master and share the exact free-variable-capture code this bug lives in -- were used instead, and DO reproduce the bug byte-for-byte (same ~1e9-magnitude garbage-double symptom, same 'argument is not a pair' corrupted-list symptom). The fix is in the shared capture-reconstruction code, so it will also cover `taylor` once that lands on master, PROVIDED taylor's own codegen reuses this same machinery (autodiff_codegen.cpp's derivative()/gradient()/resolveGradientCaptures()/the shared jacobian-family capture resolver) rather than a fully separate implementation -- worth a quick recheck when the P11 branch merges.
root_cause: codegenLambda (lib/backend/llvm_codegen.cpp) ALREADY captures a TCO loop-carried parameter's alloca (named '<param>_tco') correctly BY VALUE at closure-creation time -- both the capture-construction side (~line 26019: 'TCO alloca - load the value and capture it (not pointer)') and the lambda-body capture-consumption side (~line 25569: 'Lambda using closure env pointer for capture') agree on single-load VALUE semantics for '_tco' allocas, distinct from the pointer-marker double-indirection used for ordinary mutable let-bound captures. This convention exists specifically because bug-RR (map_codegen.cpp's isTcoLoopAlloca) already fixed the analogous problem for `map`. However, `derivative()`, `gradient()`, and the shared jacobian/hessian/divergence/curl/laplacian capture resolver (resolveGradientCaptures and its sibling in autodiff_codegen.cpp) do NOT call through the closure's already-built runtime environment when the differentiated function resolves directly to an LLVM Function* (the common case for a let-bound or inline lambda argument, via resolve_lambda_callback_). Instead they INDEPENDENTLY re-derive each free variable's storage by looking it up directly in symbol_table_/global_symbol_table_ at the derivative/gradient CALL SITE (falling back to the plain variable name when no 'lambda_capture_var' GlobalVariable convention is found -- the common case for an ordinary in-function lambda). Having found `storage`, all of these capture-reconstruction sites then chose between two forwarding conventions using ONLY `storage->getType()->isPointerTy()`: pointer-typed -> assume 'mutable let-bound variable', pack the alloca's ADDRESS as a {tag=INT64, data=ptrtoint(storage)} marker for the callee to re-dereference (double indirection); non-pointer-typed -> assume 'value-typed capture' (e.g. a struct Argument), funnel the value itself through a temp slot (single indirection, matching what the callee's '_tco' branch expects). A TCO loop parameter's alloca IS pointer-typed (every AllocaInst is), so -- lacking a check equivalent to map_codegen.cpp's isTcoLoopAlloca -- it ALWAYS fell into the pointer-marker branch. The callee, however, treats a '_tco' free variable as a single-load VALUE capture (per codegenLambda above), so it read the packed {INT64, ptrtoint(the_tco_alloca)} marker's raw bit pattern as if it were the captured double directly -- i.e. it read the STACK ADDRESS of the TCO alloca, reinterpreted as a number (explaining the observed ~6e9-magnitude garbage: a plausible stack address) or as a corrupted list-cell pointer (explaining 'car/cdr: argument is not a pair' and spurious SIGSEGVs misreported as stack overflow).
fix: lib/backend/autodiff_codegen.cpp: added a local `isTcoLoopAlloca(Value*)` helper (mirrors map_codegen.cpp's, same '_tco' suffix check) in the file's anonymous namespace, with a doc comment explaining the value-vs-pointer capture-convention mismatch. Applied it as a new branch, checked BEFORE the existing pointer/non-pointer split, at all four live free-variable-capture reconstruction sites: (1) derivative()'s capture loop, (2) gradient()'s capture loop, (3) the shared jacobian/hessian/divergence/curl/laplacian capture resolver, (4) resolveGradientCaptures(). In each case, when `storage` is a TCO loop alloca, the fix LOADS the current iteration's value from the alloca and funnels that VALUE (not its address) through a temp slot, exactly mirroring the pre-existing 'value-typed capture' branch instead of the 'mutable pointer' branch. A fifth occurrence inside the #if 0'd `derivativeStaticOnly` (dead code kept for reference, superseded by codegenDerivativeMonolith per the AD-1 note in the same file) was left untouched since it does not compile.
verification: Minimal repros (scalar via inline lambda, scalar via let-bound lambda, list-valued capture, all via `derivative`; scalar via `gradient` on a vector-argument lambda) confirmed producing garbage/crashing pre-fix and correct values post-fix, under both -r and AOT. Full regression: tests/closures/closure_edge_test.esk (40/40), tests/closures/shared_mutable_capture_test.esk (24/24), scripts/run_tco_tests.sh + tests/control_flow/tco_validation_test.esk, scripts/run_sicp_smoke.sh (88/88 gate probes), scripts/run_ad_oracle.sh (56/56), scripts/run_autodiff_tests.sh (54/54), tests/ad/{nested_ad_test,nested_ad_loop_test,mixed_mode_ad_test,reverse_nested_forward_test,stateful_tape_test}.esk all pass unchanged.
deliverable_test: tests/closures/tco_loop_capture_test.esk -- covers: a plain (non-AD) closure call over a TCO loop-carried parameter as a regression guard (this path was already correct); `derivative` on an inline lambda over a TCO loop scalar parameter (un-workaround'd, no fresh-local rebind); `derivative` on a let-bound closure over the same; `derivative` on a TCO loop LIST-valued capture; `gradient` on a let-bound closure over a TCO loop scalar parameter; and a non-recursive baseline to guard against an overly broad fix breaking the non-TCO case.
